Manufacturing Operations

Instructor Details

Guy Curry

Email:  g-curry@tamu.edu

Office Location:  ETB 4009

Phone:  979-571-2996 (cell)

Office Hours

Before class from about 12:30-2:00 and after class by arrangement in classroom.

Preferred Contact Method

email: g-curry@tamu.edu

Catalog Description

Manufacturing Operations. (3-0). Credit 3. Analytical principles of manufacturing

systems design, analysis and control; emphasis placed on stochastic analysis; role of

variability and impact on cycle time; push versus pull production strategies including

Kanban and constant WIP control; probability, queuing theory, Little’s Law, heavy

traffic approximations, and queuing networks. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in

ISEN 340 and MATH 304; junior or senior classification.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in ISEN 340 and MATH 304;

junior or senior classification.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

develop and solve analytical models of push production systems at the

operational level

develop and solve analytical models of pull/CONWIP production systems at

the operational level

develop and solve analytical models of pull/Kanban production systems at the

operational level

Special Course Designation

None

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Recommended

Manufacturing Systems Modeling and Analysis Second Edition

ISBN:  978-3-642-16617-4

Authors:  Guy L. Curry and Richard M. Feldman

Publisher:  Springer

Edition:  2nd

Notes:

Available for free download from Canvas Week 1 Materials

Grading Policy

Homework: 0%

Exam 1: 1/3% (Thursday of Week 4, February 5 )

Exam 2: 1/3% (Thursday of Week 8, March 5) The week before Spring Break.

Exam 3: 1/3% (Thursday, April. 9)

Exam 4: 1/3% (the final period is scheduled for 1-3 pm, Tuesday, May 5)

Note: Best three of the four exams will be used.

Grading Policy

Homework: 0%

Exam 1: 1/3% (Thursday of Week 4, February 5 )

Exam 2: 1/3% (Thursday of Week 8, March 5) The week before Spring Break.

Exam 3: 1/3% (Thursday, April. 9)

Exam 4: 1/3% (the final period is scheduled for 1-3 pm, Tuesday, May 5)

Note: Best three of the four exams will be used.

Grades assigned are A for 90%–100%, B for 80%–89.9%, C for 70%–79.9%, D for

60%–69.9% and F for less than 60%.

Due to no grading support for this class, I will not pickup and grade homework

assignments. Homework is a major learning experience and is meant to help

prepare you for the exams. As such, I do not expect homework to be done

independently. A suggestion for the homework process is to do each problem on

your own, then check with other students as to the correctness of your solution,

and when incorrect, use that information as an opportunity to improve your

knowledge in this course. We will cover questions and short discussions of the

homework solutions at the start of each lecture period. If something is not

understood that a good time to visit my office or to setup a Zoom help session for

clarifications or ask about it during our weekly Zoom help sessions.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).
